subject: Ecology
title: Species-Area Relationship
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Species-Area Relationship.

hook.text: Double the island's area and you get roughly 25% more species—a universal pattern.

buildup.text: The species-area curve appears on islands, continents, and even petri dishes.

discovery.text: Larger areas offer more habitats, more resources, and larger populations less prone to extinction.

twist.text: When habitats shrink, the curve predicts how many species will be lost—a conservation alarm.

climax.text: Halving a forest's area doesn't lose half the species—but it can lose 10-15% of them.

punchline.text: More space means more species. Always.

quiz.question: What happens to species when habitat area is halved?
options: About 10-15% of species may be lost
